strategy: Attack with Myth, Life, or Death; each does +30%. Avoid Fire, Ice, Storm, and Balance.
Do not cast Traps. In both forms he answers a Trap with Cleanse Ward, and in Shadow form he adds an all-enemy Shadow Trap on you.
Set up with blades and auras instead of traps, and note he Hexes all enemies each Round.
Burst down the Balance form; when it dies he Polymorphs into a Shadow form that summons two random minions next Round.
In the Shadow phase use AoE to clear the summoned minions, which cheat-cast Infection, Disarm, Steal Pip, and more; he resummons when all are dead.
Kill minions in the same Round when possible so his every-fourth-round Sacrifice heal (2,000) has fewer targets to convert.
cheatNotes: Any Trap you place is wiped by his Balance Cleanse Ward, so set up with blades instead.
At the start of every third Round he casts a random spell from the list below, including a 4-pip Loremaster or an Efreet with a -90% damage charm; keep a heal ready.
When his Balance form dies it does not end; he Polymorphs into a Shadow form with new cheats, so save AoE for the second phase.
On his first Round as the Shadow form he summons two random minions that then cheat every Round; plan AoE to thin them fast.
In Shadow form a Trap gets cleansed and he drops a Shadow Trap on your whole team; keep avoiding traps.
Every fourth Round after summoning he sacrifices a minion to heal 2,000; kill minions yourself so he has fewer to convert.
Clearing every minion just makes him resummon next Round, so time your kill on him for the same Round you clear the board.
Flames of Doom can Mass Infect your team, cutting your healing; carry Cleanse Charm.
History of Horrors can Disarm a random wizard, losing your queued card.
Malefic Mythology can steal a pip from a random wizard, slowing your setup.
Savage Stories can hit the team with Blizzard.
Terrors of the Deep either buffs Bodleian with Power Current or Confuses a wizard for one Round.
The Road to Ruin can hit the team with Leafstorm.
The Woeful Wastes can Power Link a random wizard, healing itself off the drain.
